title: Phyllagathis fruticosa
intro: Phyllagathis fruticosa, also known as the Himalayan forest mint, is a plant species that belongs to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Phyllagathis fruticosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phyllagathis fruticosa is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or powder, and is used to make herbal teas, tonics, and capsules for the treatment of various health conditions.
